items.title: Trump vows to blockade Strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks stumble
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump on Sunday said the U.S. navy would immediately start blockading the Strait of Hormuz and would also interdict every vessel in international waters that had paid a toll to Iran.

items.title: Ukraine, Russia accuse each other of violating Orthodox Easter ceasefire
items.summary_auto: Russia and Ukraine accused each other on Sunday of breaching the 32-hour ceasefire in their four-year war, each reporting close to 2,000 drone and shelling attacks just hours after the truce began on Saturday to mark Orthodox Easter.

items.title: Hungarians vote in landmark election that could unseat Orbán
items.summary_auto: Orbán, a Eurosceptic nationalist, has carved out a model of an "illiberal democracy" seen as a blueprint by Trump's Make  America Great Again (MAGA) movement and its admirers in Europe.

items.title: Fuel protests in Ireland continue as pumps run dry, prices rise amid war in Middle East
items.summary_auto: Trucks and tractors continued to block access to vital fuel depots and a major port, and vehicles clogging traffic led to closures of part of the main highway around Dublin, as well as sections of other major roadways.

items.title: A novelist was accused of using AI. Why the literary world is still grappling with guardrails
items.summary_auto: The recent cancelling of a horror novelist's book demonstrates the conundrum that literary professionals face as they comb through every pitch, query letter and manuscript lobbed their way: how do you separate the proverbial wheat from the AI-generated chaff, and what happens if you get it wrong?

items.title: The R-word was taboo as a slur for people with disabilities. Now it's being normalized again
items.summary_auto: The R-word has been considered taboo for several years, as a slur against people with intellectual and developmental disabilities. Now, the word appears to be undergoing a resurgence and normalization.

items.title: CAQ to pick new leader and Quebec premier today
In a new CUSMA, should Canada offer the U.S. stronger energy rights?
items.summary_auto: With the future of free trade between Canada and the United States unclear at best, a look to the past could provide hints at where critical exports of energy fit into a deal in the future.

items.title: There's a silver lining in the Iran war ceasefire for Canada's oil and gas industry
items.summary_auto: The Iran war appears to have ended (if it has ended) with a settlement that neither fully opens the Strait of Hormuz nor keeps it fully closed. The lingering doubts about the Persian Gulf as a source of energy for Europe and Asia, especially if Iran succeeds in its goal of charging tolls for passage, could be good news for Canadian oil and gas — if disaster can be averted.

items.title: Quebec to get new premier today as CAQ leadership race concludes
items.summary_auto: The leadership race to replace outgoing Quebec Premier François Legault concludes Sunday and the results are set to be announced in Drummondville, Que., in the afternoon.

items.title: Viktor Orbán spent 16 years building Hungary's 'illiberal' democracy. On Sunday, he may be voted out
McIlroy stumbles at the Masters, shares lead with Cameron Young entering final round
items.summary_auto: Rory McIlroy's march to another Masters title took a ride through the Georgia pines and into the water and all sorts of places he didn't want to be Saturday, including having to share space at the top of the leaderboard with Cameron Young.

items.title: Rangers end 'majestic' snowmobile trek through Canada's North in Churchill
items.summary_auto: Members of the 1st Canadian Ranger Patrol Group have arrived to their final destination after a 52-day exercise that took them all across the country's North.

items.title: Blue Jays' injury woes continue as Springer fractures big toe
items.summary_auto: The relentless injury bug continues to hit the Toronto Blue Jays hard. The latest blow saw George Springer suffer a broken left big toe in Saturday's 7-4 loss to the visiting Minnesota Twins.

items.title: 'Breaks my heart': Investigation launched after black bear found dead on Sunshine Coast road
items.summary_auto: The B.C. Conservation Officer Service said as the investigation is ongoing, no further information can be provided at this time.

items.title: Pro-Palestinian organizers end Giller Prize boycott, citing successful campaign
items.summary_auto: CanLit Responds says the group received confirmation in the form of an email from executive director Elana Rabinovitch that the Giller no longer has sponsorship ties to Scotiabank or the Azrieli Foundation.
